title: Add Parent Portal: Login, Dashboard, Child Progress View & Comment System

requirements.summary: Add parent account management to localStorage with parent-child linking and separate session handling
acceptanceCriteria: Parent account creation stores parentUsername and childUsername with child's hashed password as credential
parentLogin() validates parentUsername + childPassword against the linked student's stored password
If childPassword does not match the linked student's password, login fails with an error message
getLinkedChildData() returns all student data namespaced under the child's username from localStorage
Parent session is stored separately from student session so both can coexist without collision

file_operations.description: Add parent account storage structure with fields { parentUsername, passwordHash, childUsername }. Implement createParentAccount() to validate child account exists and password matches before creating parent entry. Add parentLogin() to authenticate by validating parentUsername and childPassword against the linked student's stored password hash. Implement getParentSession(), setParentSession(), and clearParentSession() using a separate sessionStorage key ('parentSession') to avoid collision with student session ('userSession'). Add getLinkedChildData(parentUsername) that retrieves the parent record, extracts childUsername, and returns the complete child profile and all namespaced data (subjects, chapters, notes, questions, mockTests with attempts, flashcards with learned status, questionBank, plannerTasks, targets, studyStreak, revisionTasks, reminders, progressSummary). Ensure all parent-related functions throw descriptive errors for invalid credentials or missing child accounts.

requirements.summary: Add parent login and registration UI to the LoginPage with modal dialogs for authentication
acceptanceCriteria: 'Login as Parent' and 'Create Parent Account' buttons are visible on the login page below the student auth options
'Create Parent Account' dialog collects Parent Username, Child's Username, and Child's Password
If child username does not exist or password is wrong, show a clear error message
On successful parent login, user is redirected to the Parent Dashboard page
Parent auth state is tracked separately from student auth state

file_operations.description: Add two new buttons below the existing student login/register buttons: 'Login as Parent' and 'Create Parent Account'. Implement 'Create Parent Account' dialog using shadcn/ui Dialog component with Input fields for Parent Username, Child's Username, and Child's Password. On submission, call createParentAccount() from localStorageService and handle errors (child not found, password mismatch) with clear toast/alert messages. Implement 'Login as Parent' dialog with fields for Parent Username and Child's Password. On successful parentLogin(), call setParentSession() and use useNavigate() to redirect to '/parent-dashboard'. Add state management for dialog visibility and form validation. Style dialogs consistently with existing login/register UI using Tailwind classes.

requirements.summary: Create Parent Dashboard page with personalized child data display, performance summary, and comment system
acceptanceCriteria: Parent dashboard route /parent-dashboard is accessible only when a parent session is active; redirects to login otherwise
Parent username is displayed at top of page; child's name is displayed directly beneath it
All section headings and descriptions use 'Your son/daughter [Name]' phrasing
Overall performance card shows chapter completion %, average mock test score, current streak, and targets met
Subjects section lists all child's subjects with chapter count and completion percentage
Mock Tests section shows each test with Attempted/Not Attempted status, attempt count, and best score
Flashcards section shows total count and per-card flip/review count
Question Bank section shows all questions grouped by subject/chapter
Comment box is prominently placed with Scold/Comment/Appreciate buttons that pre-fill textarea tone prefix
Send button is disabled when textarea is empty

file_operations.description: Create a new Parent Dashboard page component. At the top, display parent username from getParentSession() and child's name from getLinkedChildData() with labels 'Parent: [parentUsername]' and 'Viewing: [Child's Name]'. Use personalized phrasing 'Your son/daughter [Name]' throughout (default to 'child' if gender not stored). Implement sections: (1) Overall Performance card with chapter completion percentage (completedChapters/totalChapters), average mock test score from test attempts, current streak from studyStreak, and targets achieved count. (2) Subjects grid showing each subject with chapter count and completion bars. (3) Notes section listing note counts per subject/chapter with expandable note titles. (4) Questions section showing question counts per chapter. (5) Mock Tests table with columns: Test Name, Subject, Status (Attempted/Not Attempted based on testAttempts), Attempt Count, Best Score. (6) Flashcards section showing total count and per-card learned/review stats. (7) Question Bank table grouped by subject/chapter with question text preview. (8) Comment Box prominently centered with heading 'Send feedback to [Child's Name]', three styled buttons ('Scold 😠' in red, 'Comment 💬' in blue, 'Appreciate 🌟' in green) that pre-fill textarea with tone prefix ('⚠️ [Scold]: ', '💬 [Comment]: ', '🌟 [Appreciate]: '). Add textarea for message and Send button (disabled when empty). On Send, call addNotification() targeting child's username with notification object { id: Date.now(), from: parentUsername, type, message, timestamp: Date.now(), read: false }, clear textarea, and show success toast. Use shadcn/ui Card, Table, Badge, Button, Textarea components. Fetch all data via getLinkedChildData() on mount. Style with Tailwind using gradient headers and responsive grid layouts.

requirements.summary: Implement notification system in localStorage and add notification bell to student Layout
acceptanceCriteria: Sending a comment from the parent dashboard writes a notification entry to the child's localStorage namespace
Student Layout shows a bell icon in the header/sidebar with a red badge showing unread notification count
Badge disappears or shows 0 when all notifications are read
Notification panel lists each message with sender, type (Scold/Comment/Appreciate) badge, message body, and timestamp
Clicking a notification marks it read and removes it from the unread count
Appreciate notifications are styled in green, Comment in blue, Scold in red/orange
After sending a comment, the parent dashboard textarea is cleared and a success confirmation is shown

file_operations.description: Add notification management functions: addNotification(username, notification: { id: number, from: string, type: 'scold'|'comment'|'appreciate', message: string, timestamp: number, read: boolean }) that appends the notification to the user's namespaced notifications array in localStorage. Implement getNotifications(username) that retrieves all notifications for the user. Add markNotificationRead(username, notificationId) that finds the notification by id and sets read to true. Ensure notifications are stored under the key pattern 'notifications_[username]' for proper namespacing.

Add a notification bell icon (using lucide-react Bell icon) to the sidebar header next to the logout/login button. Fetch notifications via getNotifications(currentUsername) and calculate unread count. Display a red badge with the count if > 0. Implement a dropdown/popover (using shadcn/ui Popover or DropdownMenu) that opens on bell click, listing all notifications. Each notification item shows sender name ('From: [parentUsername]'), type badge (Scold in red-orange, Comment in blue, Appreciate in green), message text, and formatted timestamp. Add onClick handler to each notification that calls markNotificationRead() and updates local state to remove the badge. Use useEffect to poll or refresh notifications when the component mounts or when navigating to Layout. Style the bell icon with relative positioning for the badge overlay and the dropdown with max-height and scroll for long lists.

requirements.summary: Add protected /parent-dashboard route to TanStack Router with auth guard and logout functionality
acceptanceCriteria: /parent-dashboard route exists and renders the Parent Dashboard page
Navigating to /parent-dashboard without a parent session redirects to /login
A logout button on the parent dashboard clears parent session and navigates to /login
All existing student routes remain unaffected

file_operations.description: Import ParentDashboard component. Add a new route object for '/parent-dashboard' in the routesConfig array with element: <ParentDashboard />. Implement a beforeLoad guard for this route that checks getParentSession() from localStorageService; if no parent session exists, redirect to '/login' using navigate({ to: '/login' }). Ensure the route is registered in the router configuration alongside existing student routes. Do not modify any existing route guards or student route definitions.

Add a logout button in the top-right corner of the Parent Dashboard header. Import useNavigate from TanStack Router. On button click, call clearParentSession() from localStorageService and navigate to '/login' using navigate({ to: '/login' }). Style the logout button consistently with existing UI using shadcn/ui Button component with variant='outline'.
